intro.title: RUNTIME AS THIN AS AIR.
intro.description: Zenith keeps the browser surface literal. Runtime receives emitted markers, binding tables, and explicit setup boundaries, then executes that work without rediscovering structure.

hydrationContract.description: Hydration consumes explicit payload tables with stable marker, expression, and event ordering.
hydrationContract.note: Discovery passes are banned. Fallback behavior is banned. If payload order drifts, runtime throws instead of improvising.

principles.title: Hydrate by markers, not discovery.
principles.intro: Compiler-authored marker and event tables tell runtime exactly where to bind. The browser does not scan DOM structure to recover intent.
principles.sideText: Marker, expression, and event indices arrive in compiler order so runtime can bind the exact nodes it was handed.
principles.mainTitle: Compiler emission fixes marker, expression, and event ordering before hydration starts. The browser receives positions, not a structure to infer.
principles.mainText: Binding is positional and deterministic. Runtime attaches work to the nodes the compiler already chose instead of searching the DOM for semantic intent.

support.label: Failure Mode
support.text: If marker order or payload order drifts, runtime throws. There is no recovery pass and no silent fallback path.

support.label: Discovery Status
support.text: Banned. The browser never walks the tree to guess handler targets, component ownership, or reactive scope.

principles.title: Execute payloads. Do not reinterpret them.
principles.intro: Runtime executes emitted artifacts and explicit mount boundaries. It does not parse expression strings or rebuild a framework layer after build.
principles.sideText: Artifacts arrive precomputed. Browser work is dispatch, mount, and update over emitted payloads.
principles.mainTitle: Runtime executes deterministic payload work. It does not interpret strings or recover structure the compiler already decided.
principles.mainText: eval, new Function, and hidden framework lifecycle layers are outside the contract.

support.label: Allowed
support.text: hydrate(payload), zenMount(), and zeneffect(...) as explicit boundaries.

support.label: Banned
support.text: Runtime parsing, semantic recovery, and framework-style discovery between emitted output and the DOM.

principles.title: Keep runtime work in explicit primitives and refs.
principles.intro: Bindings stay positional, primitives stay explicit, and browser work stays scoped to ref<T>() and zenMount(...).
principles.sideText: Refs, signals, and explicit helpers keep browser work narrow, inspectable, and deterministic.
principles.mainTitle: Runtime never resolves identifiers by name. Signals, lowered state, refs, and setup boundaries stay explicit all the way to the browser.
principles.mainText: Use zenOn(), zenResize(), and collectRefs(...refs) instead of raw global scans or ad hoc listener wiring.
